rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LOEW ET AL VS. ALICE M. TRAEG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award And For Entry Of Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 31, 2021 line 1. PETITIONERS GEORGE LOEW, ANDREW LUSARDI, JOSEPH MALLON, JANE FRESTON, FLORENCE SURATT AND MARTIN MILLER'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON AMENDED PETITION TO CONFIRM CONTRACTUAL ARBITRATION AWARD AND FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT THEREON is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: PATRICK FERRY VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 31, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the first cause of action for interference with easement is sustained without leave to amend; no substantive opposition filed addressing issues raised by demurrer. Demurrer to the second cause of action for inverse condemnation is overruled. Moving party failed to cite any authority for the proposition that tree roots (the subject of this complaint) are not inherently dangerous as a matter of law. City of Oroville v. Superior Court (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1091 is inapposite having addressed the inherent dangers of the sewer construction, maintenance and design at trial. Moving party to prepare order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HENRY YIP VS. AYANA BASTAIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 31, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT GEORGENA BELL'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required. Claimant to bring all evidence supporting her claim that she was in possession as of February 20, 2021 per a written agreement with the landlord and with someone other than the landlord, including but not limited to copies of the rental agreement, rent payment receipts, cancelled checks, utility bills, school enrollment, paychecks or government assistance checks reflecting the address at the premises prior to February 20, 2020, etc. All evidence to be provided to the courtroom clerks no later than 9:00 a.m. on August 31, 2021 and filed in room 103 by 9:30 a.m. on August 31, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH HURLEY VS. ESTHER DEFRUTOS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 31, 2021 line 3. DEFENDANT ESTHER DEFRUTOS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Assuming (without so deciding) that SFRO 37.9(n) applies, Plaintiff's UD is based on a safety issue. Whether presence of unauthorized strangers in the building's garage and common areas raises safety concerns for occupants is an issue of fact not appropriate to be addressed on a demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
